disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Painful discrete oval sores confined to the inside of the lower lip and cheeks, sparing the roof of the mouth, gums and tongue
trigger_question_examples: Which parts of your mouth are affected?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're on the inside of my lower lip and the inside of my cheeks. The roof of my mouth, my gums and my tongue feel fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Onset began with a tingling prodrome, with no blisters or vesicles preceding the sores
trigger_question_examples: Did you notice any blisters before the sores appeared?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started as a tingling feeling on my lip, then the sores came up. I didn't have any blisters first.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent similar episodes every few months, typically during periods of stress
trigger_question_examples: Have you had sores like this before?
Does anything seem to bring them on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I get them every few months, usually when I'm stressed. This is just the worst it's ever been.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever and no tender or swollen neck glands
trigger_question_examples: Are the glands in your neck swollen or sore?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, and my neck feels normal — no lumps or soreness that I've noticed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Has not been able to take any fluids for around 18 hours because swallowing is too painful
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to drink anything?
When did you last manage a drink?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I haven't managed anything to drink for about 18 hours — every time I try to swallow it's agony.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Passing a little less urine than usual, no dizziness on standing at present
trigger_question_examples: Are you still passing urine normally?
Do you feel dizzy or lightheaded when you stand up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I passed urine earlier, maybe a bit less than usual, and I don't feel dizzy standing up so far.
